nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; adults tend to inhabit lagoon and seaward reefs, often close to the bottom among rubble beds; depth range approximately 1–20 m

diet.notes: Has been documented feeding on zooplankton and, to a lesser extent, benthic algae in the wild. In captivity, have been known to accept a wide variety of meaty foods, herbivore preparations, and flaked foods.

summary: A vibrant electric-blue-and-yellow damselfish from the Indo-Pacific, generally considered beginner-friendly but have been known to be confrontational toward tankmates; best kept singly in a reef with ample hiding spots.
careNotes:
The Blue and Gold Damselfish displays electric blue on the upper body with bright yellow on the lower body and posterior — coloration can vary considerably between individuals. They tend to spend much of their time darting in and out of holes and crevices, particularly in well-lit tanks. Despite being reef-safe, they have been reported to be aggressive toward other fish, especially conspecifics and other damselfish; keeping them singly is generally advised. In the wild, juveniles have been documented schooling among soft corals while adults form small groups or larger congregations near rubble beds.

Water parameter cross-source notes: Temperature averaged from LiveAquaria (22.2–25.6 °C), TankFacts (24.4–26.7 °C), and FishBase wild data (24.9–29.3 °C) → averaged to 23.8–27.2 °C. Salinity averaged from LiveAquaria (1.020–1.025), TankFacts (1.023–1.026), and FreshMarine (1.020–1.025) → 1.021–1.025. pH averaged from LiveAquaria (8.1–8.4), TankFacts (7.8–8.4), and FreshMarine (8.1–8.4) → 8.0–8.4. Minimum tank volume averaged from LiveAquaria (30 gal / ~114 L) and FreshMarine (55 gal / ~208 L) → ~161 L. Adult max size averaged from LiveAquaria (3.5 in / 8.89 cm), FreshMarine (up to 4 in / 10.16 cm), and FishBase (9.0 cm) → ~9.4 cm max.
breedingNotes: In the wild, this species is oviparous with distinct pairing behavior; males have been documented guarding and aerating eggs. Captive breeding has not been widely reported in the aquarium hobby literature consulted.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (9.0 cm TL), temperature range (24.9–29.3 °C wild), native range, taxonomy, and behavior. Designated secondary source.

additional.name: TankFacts
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (76–80 °F / 24.4–26.7 °C), salinity (1.023–1.026), and pH (7.8–8.4). Used in numerical averaging for water parameters.

additional.name: FreshMarine
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (72–78 °F), salinity (1.020–1.025), pH (8.1–8.4), tank size (55 gal), and adult size (up to 4 in). Used in numerical averaging.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous; distinct pairing observed in the wild with male egg-guarding behavior. Captive breeding has rarely been documented in the hobby.
